front: Novel: opens "This is the saddest story I have ever heard"
Novel: narrated by unreliable narrator John Dowell about Edward Ashburnham
Novel: Nancy Rufford becomes catatonic, can only say 'shuttlecocks' and a Latin prayer; sent to Ceylon
Novel: Florence fakes a heart ailment and commits suicide by drinking prussic acid after her affair is exposed
Novel: set at the spa town of Nauheim where characters seek treatment for heart ailments
Novel: Edward Ashburnham slits his throat with a penknife; telegram 'Safe Brindisi. Having a rattling good time'
Novel: John Rodker called it "the finest French novel in the English language"
Novel: originally titled 'The Saddest Story'; excerpted in first issue of Wyndham Lewis's BLAST
Novel: La Dolciquita is a Spanish dancer after the title character's money
Novel: "the last Tory" Christopher Tietjens works as a statistician before serving in WWI; his wife Sylvia plots to cut down the Great Tree of Groby
Novel: Tietjens has an affair with suffragette Valentine Wannop; they meet after suffragettes harass a minister on a golf course
Novel: third volume A Man Could Stand Up-- set on Armistice Day; skylark nearly flies into Tietjens's mouth during battle
Novel: Tom Stoppard wrote the screenplay for a 2012 BBC adaptation starring Benedict Cumberbatch
Novel: Welsh soldier O Nine Morgan killed by mortar shell; haunts the protagonist
Author: collaborated with Joseph Conrad on The Inheritors (Fourth Dimensionists plot to take over Earth), Romance, and The Nature of a Crime
Author: founded The English Review and The Transatlantic Review
Author: championed literary impressionism; admired impressionism of Stephen Crane's Red Badge of Courage
Author: Jean Rhys fictionalized her relationship with this author in Quartet (also published as Postures)
Author: Hemingway described this author as a 'well-clothed, up-ended hogshead' in A Moveable Feast
Author: wrote The Fifth Queen trilogy about Catherine Howard; Privy Seal is the second volume
